title: Fracttalix Sentinel: Three-Channel Streaming Anomaly Detector
description: Fracttalix: a research corpus comprising the Fractal Rhythm Model (FRM), Meta-Kaizen series, and the Dual Reader Standard (DRS). The FRM describes association dynamics via f(t) = B + A·exp(−λt)·cos(ωt+φ) across scales from quantum decoherence to cosmological structure. The Meta-Kaizen series (6 papers) derives a measurement-theoretic governance framework from six axioms. The DRS is a verification architecture comprising two protocols: the Dual Reader Protocol (DRP) for text and the Grounded Verification Protocol (GVP) for software. The DRS Architecture specification formally defines the complete standard including the Falsification Kernel K=(P,O,M,B), six verification tiers, and the machine lingua franca property. Every claim carries a machine-evaluable falsification predicate that reduces to embedded binary logic — a deterministic single-bit verdict independent of human language. Includes Fracttalix Sentinel v12.3, a three-channel streaming anomaly detector implementing the FRM (zero dependencies, 2.6% normal FPR at production defaults). 23 corpus objects, 162 machine-readable claims with falsification predicates, 21 AI layers, and full DRS compliance.
notes: v12.3.0: FPR elimination (35%→2.6%) and drift recovery. SeasonalPreprocessStep (FFT deseasonalization), ConsensusGate (≥2 soft alerts required), VarCUSUM/CUSUM k correction (0.5→1.0), null-distribution threshold recalibration, non-adaptive drift CUSUM. Mean F1 +25% (0.500→0.625). 405 tests passing. Theoretical foundation: Fractal Rhythm Model (FRM) — 11 axioms. Meta-Kaizen series — 6 papers deriving KVS from measurement-theoretic axioms and extending the DRS to software. Dual Reader Standard (DRS) — verification architecture with DRP (text protocol) and GVP (software protocol), with standalone Architecture Specification. Falsification Kernel K=(P,O,M,B) reduces every claim to embedded binary logic. 23 corpus objects across two tracks. 162 claims, 21 AI layers, schema v3-S51. Introduced by Thomas Brennan with AI collaborators Claude (Anthropic) and Grok (xAI).
